text:
Market today: Tech is bouncing back after a chip-stock selloff, but the recovery feels fragile. The real driver is AI infrastructure anxiety - costs, energy bills, and demand sustainability are all being questioned at once.

Your holdings: AVGO (-12.89%, your largest position) is bleeding on margin - a continued chip selloff hits this hardest and fastest. PLTR (-10.31%) is also in the red and flagged SELL, meaning your scanner wants you out - sitting on a loss with no catalyst visible is a bad combo on margin. SPCX is a brand-new IPO with almost no price history - tiny moves feel big and margin amplifies every tick against you; manage it by hand and keep the leash short.

Watch: Whether today's tech rebound has legs or fades into the close - a weak close after a bounce is a warning sign. Also watch any Congressional movement on forcing AI companies to pay their own energy costs; that's a direct headwind for your chip and cloud names.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers (AVGO and PLTR) are both on margin - either cut or set tight stops before this week is out.
